metadata.name: EP-133 K.O. II
metadata.description: 64 MB sampler and composer with 999 sample slots, 16 voice polyphony, song mode, and punch-in FX 2.0. Features hands-free sampling, resampling, and comprehensive MIDI connectivity.

tooltip.title: Output (3.5mm Stereo)
tooltip.description: Main stereo audio output. Connect to speakers, mixer, or audio interface using a 3.5mm TRS cable.
tooltip.tip: This is a line-level output. Use the VOLUME knob to adjust output level.

tooltip.title: Input (3.5mm Stereo)
tooltip.description: Audio input for sampling. Supports line-level signals and microphone with auto-gain. Orange color indicates input section.
tooltip.tip: Use GAIN knob to adjust input level. The display shows input level during sampling.

tooltip.title: Sync (3.5mm)
tooltip.description: Analog sync input/output for connecting to Pocket Operators and other Teenage Engineering devices. Supports 8 PPM, 16 PPM, and 24 ppqn modes.
tooltip.tip: Configure sync mode in System menu (SHIFT + ERASE).

tooltip.title: MIDI (3.5mm TRS)
tooltip.description: MIDI input and output via 3.5mm TRS connector. MMA-compliant Type-A wiring. Use TRS-to-DIN adapters for standard MIDI connections.
tooltip.tip: OS 2.0 adds MIDI Thru functionality and support for up to 16 devices.

tooltip.title: USB-C
tooltip.description: USB-C port for power, MIDI over USB, firmware updates, and sample transfer via the EP-133 app.
tooltip.tip: Can be powered via USB while using batteries as backup.

tooltip.title: Power Switch
tooltip.description: Slide to turn the EP-133 on or off. The device runs on 4x AAA batteries or USB power.
tooltip.tip: Battery life is approximately 8 hours with AAA batteries.

tooltip.title: Built-in Speaker
tooltip.description: Mono speaker for monitoring without headphones. Disabled when headphones or line output is connected.
tooltip.tip: The speaker is useful for quick previews but use headphones or monitors for critical listening.

tooltip.title: Built-in Microphone
tooltip.description: Internal microphone for quick sampling when no external source is connected.
tooltip.tip: Use the external input for better quality recordings.

tooltip.title: LCD Display
tooltip.description: Shows current bar, tempo (BPM), stereo/mono indicator, sound number, FX status, quantize setting (Q), and swing amount. Display format changes based on current mode.
tooltip.tip: The BAR indicator shows your position in the pattern.

tooltip.title: Volume
tooltip.description: Controls the main output volume. This is a hardware control and does not affect recorded levels or saved settings.
tooltip.tip: White knob indicates it's part of the playback/output section.

tooltip.title: Gain (X)
tooltip.description: Controls the input level for sampling. Also functions as the X parameter knob in certain contexts. Orange color indicates it's part of the input/sampling section.
tooltip.tip: Watch the input meter on the display while adjusting. Aim for strong signal without clipping.

tooltip.title: Y Parameter
tooltip.description: Multi-purpose parameter knob. Function depends on context: filter frequency, effect parameter, swing amount, or time stretch.
tooltip.tip: In Sound Edit mode, this controls the secondary parameter for the selected function.

tooltip.title: Sound / Edit
tooltip.description: Access sound selection (tap) or sound editing mode (hold SHIFT). Edit mode allows trim, envelope, tuning, and time stretch adjustments.
tooltip.tip: In SOUND mode, use number keys + ENTER to select sounds directly.

tooltip.title: Main / Commit
tooltip.description: Returns to main view (tap) or commits/saves changes (hold). Use COMMIT after editing to save your changes permanently.
tooltip.tip: Always commit after editing samples or patterns to save your work.

tooltip.title: Tempo / Loop
tooltip.description: Tap repeatedly to set tempo by tapping, or hold to access tempo settings. SHIFT + TEMPO toggles loop mode.
tooltip.tip: Hold TEMPO and turn Y knob for fine tempo adjustment.

tooltip.title: Keys Mode
tooltip.description: Enter chromatic keyboard mode to play samples across different pitches. The number keys (0-9) become a piano keyboard.
tooltip.tip: Scale modes available: chromatic, major, minor, pentatonic, and more. Use +/- to change octave.

tooltip.title: Fader
tooltip.description: Multi-purpose fader for volume, pitch, filter, pan, or effect control. Assignable per group. Movements can be recorded into the sequencer.
tooltip.tip: Hold FADER button and select parameter type: LEVEL, PITCH, TIME, LPF, HPF, ATK, REL, PAN, TUNE, VEL, MOD.

tooltip.title: Fader Mode
tooltip.description: Hold to select which parameter the fader controls. Press number keys to choose: LEVEL, PITCH, TIME, LPF, HPF, ATK, REL, PAN, TUNE, VEL, MOD.
tooltip.tip: SHIFT + FADER resets the fader to its default position.

tooltip.title: Group A
tooltip.description: Select Group A. Each group contains 12 sounds (pads 0-9, +, -) and can have up to 99 patterns. Groups play simultaneously.
tooltip.tip: SHIFT + A mutes/unmutes Group A during playback.

tooltip.title: Group B
tooltip.description: Select Group B. Each group contains 12 sounds and can have up to 99 patterns.
tooltip.tip: SHIFT + B mutes/unmutes Group B during playback.

tooltip.title: Group C
tooltip.description: Select Group C. Each group contains 12 sounds and can have up to 99 patterns.
tooltip.tip: SHIFT + C mutes/unmutes Group C during playback.

tooltip.title: Group D
tooltip.description: Select Group D. Each group contains 12 sounds and can have up to 99 patterns.
tooltip.tip: SHIFT + D mutes/unmutes Group D during playback.

tooltip.title: Pad 7 / Level
tooltip.description: Trigger sound 7 in current group. In Edit mode (FADER): sets fader to control LEVEL.
tooltip.tip: Pressure-sensitive for velocity control.

tooltip.title: Pad 8 / Pitch
tooltip.description: Trigger sound 8 in current group. In Edit mode (FADER): sets fader to control PITCH.
tooltip.tip: Pressure-sensitive for velocity control.

tooltip.title: Pad 9 / Time
tooltip.description: Trigger sound 9 in current group. In Edit mode (FADER): sets fader to control TIME (time stretch).
tooltip.tip: Pressure-sensitive for velocity control.

tooltip.title: Sample / Chop
tooltip.description: Enter sampling mode (tap) or chop mode (hold). Chop mode splits a sample into multiple slices automatically based on transient detection.
tooltip.tip: SHIFT + SAMPLE enables resampling - record the EP-133's output as a new sample.

tooltip.title: Timing / Correct
tooltip.description: Access quantize settings (tap) or timing correction (hold). Affects how recorded notes snap to the grid.
tooltip.tip: Quantize options: OFF, 1/8, 1/16, 1/32. Use +/- to select.

tooltip.title: Pad 4 / Low Pass Filter
tooltip.description: Trigger sound 4 in current group. In Edit mode: access Low Pass Filter cutoff. In FADER mode: sets fader to control LPF.
tooltip.tip: Use Y knob to adjust LPF cutoff after selecting.

tooltip.title: Pad 5 / High Pass Filter
tooltip.description: Trigger sound 5 in current group. In Edit mode: access High Pass Filter cutoff. In FADER mode: sets fader to control HPF.
tooltip.tip: Use Y knob to adjust HPF cutoff after selecting.

tooltip.title: Pad 6 / FX Send
tooltip.description: Trigger sound 6 in current group. In Edit mode: adjust effect send amount for the current sound.
tooltip.tip: Higher values send more signal to the master effect.

tooltip.title: FX / Output
tooltip.description: Access master effects (tap) or output routing settings (hold). Effects include delay, reverb, distortion, filter, compression, and chorus.
tooltip.tip: Punch-In FX 2.0: Hold number keys 1-12 during playback to apply instant effects.

tooltip.title: Erase / System
tooltip.description: Delete notes/sounds (hold + tap pads) or access system settings (SHIFT + ERASE). System settings include MIDI, sync, and global preferences.
tooltip.tip: Hold ERASE and press a group button to clear that group's pattern.

tooltip.title: Pad 1 / Attack
tooltip.description: Trigger sound 1 in current group. In Edit mode: adjust envelope attack time. In FADER mode: sets fader to control ATK.
tooltip.tip: Attack time controls how quickly the sound reaches full volume.

tooltip.title: Pad 2 / Release
tooltip.description: Trigger sound 2 in current group. In Edit mode: adjust envelope release time. In FADER mode: sets fader to control REL.
tooltip.tip: Release time controls how long the sound takes to fade out after releasing.

tooltip.title: Pad 3 / Pan
tooltip.description: Trigger sound 3 in current group. In Edit mode: adjust stereo panning. In FADER mode: sets fader to control PAN.
tooltip.tip: Pan left (-) to right (+). Center is 0.

tooltip.title: Minus / Tune
tooltip.description: Decrease value, pattern number, or navigate backward. Triggers sound 10. In Edit mode: access TUNE (pitch) parameter. In FADER mode: sets fader to control TUNE.
tooltip.tip: Tune range is +/- 24 semitones plus fine tuning.

tooltip.title: Plus / Velocity
tooltip.description: Increase value, pattern number, or navigate forward. Triggers sound 11. In Edit mode: access VEL (velocity) parameter. In FADER mode: sets fader to control VEL.
tooltip.tip: Velocity affects how hard/loud the sound plays.

tooltip.title: Pad 0 / Modulation
tooltip.description: Trigger sound 0 in current group. In Edit mode: access MOD (modulation) parameter. In FADER mode: sets fader to control MOD.
tooltip.tip: Modulation applies LFO-style effects to the sound.

tooltip.title: Enter
tooltip.description: Confirm selection or entry. Used after entering numbers to select sounds, patterns, or confirm menu options.
tooltip.tip: Also used to confirm operations in various menus.

tooltip.title: Record
tooltip.description: Enable recording mode. Orange color matches the input section. Records pad hits and fader movements to the sequencer in real-time.
tooltip.tip: Recording is quantized based on TIMING settings. Press RECORD again to stop recording.

tooltip.title: Play / Stop
tooltip.description: Start or stop playback. Press while stopped to start from the beginning of the pattern.
tooltip.tip: SHIFT + PLAY restarts from the beginning without stopping.

tooltip.title: Shift
tooltip.description: Hold to access secondary functions on other buttons. Located in the bottom-left corner. Most buttons have a secondary function shown in smaller text.
tooltip.tip: SHIFT + number keys access parameters like EDIT functions and mute groups.

tooltip.title: BPM Indicator
tooltip.description: Flashes in time with the tempo. Shows current BPM on the display when TEMPO is held.

tooltip.title: Metronome Indicator
tooltip.description: Lit when metronome is enabled. Toggle with SHIFT + TEMPO.
tooltip.tip: The metronome helps you record in time.

tooltip.title: Swing Indicator
tooltip.description: Shows current swing amount on the display. Swing delays every other note for a groove feel.
tooltip.tip: Adjust swing with Y knob while in tempo mode.
